ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_data" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance data entry */,
  "time_seconds" INTEGER /* Time in seconds since the beginning of the data collection period */,
  "beginning_of_time_timestamp_rep1" TEXT /* Timestamp of the beginning of the time for replica 1 */,
  "beginning_of_time_timestamp_rep2" TEXT /* Timestamp of the beginning of the time for replica 2 */,
  "beginning_of_time_timestamp_rep3" TEXT /* Timestamp of the beginning of the time for replica 3 */,
  "mean_response_time_rep1" REAL /* Mean response time in seconds for replica 1 */,
  "mean_response_time_rep2" REAL /* Mean response time in seconds for replica 2 */,
  "mean_response_time_rep3" REAL /* Mean response time in seconds for replica 3 */,
  "mean_netthread_processing_time_rep1" REAL /* Mean NetThread processing time in seconds for replica 1 */,
  "mean_netthread_processing_time_rep2" REAL /* Mean NetThread processing time in seconds for replica 2 */,
  "mean_netthread_processing_time_rep3" REAL /* Mean NetThread processing time in seconds for replica 3 */,
  "mean_waiting_queue_time_rep1" REAL /* Mean waiting time in the queue in seconds for replica 1 */,
  "mean_waiting_queue_time_rep2" REAL /* Mean waiting time in the queue in seconds for replica 2 */,
  "mean_waiting_queue_time_rep3" REAL /* Mean waiting time in the queue in seconds for replica 3 */,
  "mean_worker_preprocessing_time_rep1" REAL /* Mean worker pre-processing time in seconds for replica 1 */,
  "mean_worker_preprocessing_time_rep2" REAL /* Mean worker pre-processing time in seconds for replica 2 */,
  "mean_worker_preprocessing_time_rep3" REAL /* Mean worker pre-processing time in seconds for replica 3 */,
  "mean_server_service_time_rep1" REAL /* Mean server service time in seconds for replica 1 */,
  "mean_server_service_time_rep2" REAL /* Mean server service time in seconds for replica 2 */,
  "mean_server_service_time_rep3" REAL /* Mean server service time in seconds for replica 3 */,
  "mean_worker_postprocessing_time_rep1" REAL /* Mean worker post-processing time in seconds for replica 1 */,
  "mean_worker_postprocessing_time_rep2" REAL /* Mean worker post-processing time in seconds for replica 2 */,
  "mean_worker_postprocessing_time_rep3" REAL /* Mean worker post-processing time in seconds for replica 3 */,
  "mean_queue_size_rep1" REAL /* Mean queue size for replica 1 */,
  "mean_queue_size_rep2" REAL /* Mean queue size for replica 2 */,
  "mean_queue_size_rep3" REAL /* Mean queue size for replica 3 */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server associated with this performance data */,
  "interval_id" INTEGER /* ID of the time interval associated with this performance data */,
  "data_collection_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was collected */,
  "data_collection_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the data collection process */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used for data collection */,
  "data_collection_status" TEXT /* Status of the data collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_interval_id FOREIGN KEY ("interval_id") REFERENCES time_intervals ("interval_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "replicas" (
  "replica_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server replica */,
  "replica_name" TEXT /* Name of the server replica */,
  "replica_description" TEXT /* Description of the server replica */,
  "replica_status" TEXT /* Current status of the replica */,
  "replica_location" TEXT /* Physical or virtual location of the replica */,
  "replica_version" TEXT /* Version of the software or configuration used in the replica */,
  "last_update_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update or maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("replica_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_intervals" (
  "interval_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each time interval */,
  "interval_name" TEXT /* Name of the time interval */,
  "interval_description" TEXT /* Description of the time interval */,
  "interval_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the time interval in seconds */,
  "interval_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the time interval */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interval_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "server_description" TEXT /* Description of the server */,
  "replica_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server replica */,
  "server_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "server_os" TEXT /* Operating system of the server */,
  "server_hardware" TEXT /* Hardware configuration of the server */,
  "server_status" TEXT /* Current status of the server */,
  "server_last_reboot" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last server reboot */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_servers_replica_id FOREIGN KEY ("replica_id") REFERENCES replicas ("replica_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, engineer) */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "user_access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user */,
  "user_last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "user_last_activity" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance data entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the user's access */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES performance_data ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance data entry */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., high response time, queue overflow) */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert */,
  "alert_resolved_by" TEXT /* User who resolved the alert */,
  "alert_resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES performance_data ("performance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "report_length" INTEGER /* Length of the report */,
  "report_generator" TEXT /* User who generated the report */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report */,
  "report_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_report_generator FOREIGN KEY ("report_generator") REFERENCES users ("user_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_report_date FOREIGN KEY ("report_date") REFERENCES time_intervals ("interval_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "setting_description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "setting_category" TEXT /* Category of the setting */,
  "setting_last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the setting */,
  "setting_updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)
